runs.content:
**Investigation idea:** Games' prototype scaffold exists only in a memory note, never reached the actual wiki page. memory/index.md's Domain Content Status for Games says '4 prototype stubs in raw/games/: Mobile Wood Chopping Game, Children's Financial Literacy Game (+Lightfin crossover !todo), Crypto Portfolio Tracker, Project Planning Interface' — but raw/games/ doesn't currently exist as a folder, and games/prototypes.md's table is still the single placeholder '[to be added]' row it's had since 2026-05-01. The four prototype names and their one-line descriptions are sitting nowhere except that memory line. Next step: check with Fred whether those four files were ever created (and later moved/deleted) or the memory note is simply stale; either way, transcribe the four descriptions into games/prototypes.md's table.

**Cross-domain connection 1:** Finance already has the fully-built Kelly Criterion page that Sports Betting's own bankroll-management stub is explicitly waiting for. sports-betting/bankroll-management.md lists 'Kelly Criterion — optimal bet size given edge and odds' and 'Fractional Kelly' as the top two items under 'Key Concepts (to be built out),' unchanged since 2026-05-01. finance/concepts/concept-kelly-criterion.md already has the full f*=(bp−q)/b formula, a documented Half-Kelly practice (~75% volatility reduction for ~25% growth cost), and a full mispricing trade-process checklist — built out 2026-05-26 from book-a-man-for-all-markets. Neither page references the other. The Sports Betting stub doesn't need new research to close this section; it needs a translation pass on content the wiki already owns.

**Cross-domain connection 2:** Moneyball is the one book in the library that's actually about Sports Betting's stated mission, and it's filed only under Finance. book-moneyball.md's own 'Why Notable' section frames the book as being about 'finding value where others aren't looking' — exploiting a market's statistical mispricing of talent — and cross-references only Finance overview and Investment ideas. sports-betting/overview.md's stated focus is 'using AI and data-driven models to find edge in sports betting markets... systematic, model-informed betting,' close to a direct restatement of Beane/DePodesta's approach one domain over. Sports Betting has never referenced the one book in the wiki that's the closest real-world analog to what the domain is trying to build.

**Cross-domain connection 1:** The Workday disruption debate's boldest claim is contradicted by AI & Tech's own enterprise-adoption survey data, unlinked. work/source-workday-disruption-debate-2026.md's a16z thesis argues AI-native Workday could 'deploy in 1 month' and treats agentic AI ARR as near-term-achievable disruption evidence; ai-tech/data-ai-enterprise-adoption-economist-2026-06-27.md (Economist/Databricks 2026 survey of 1,221 executives) independently finds ~3 in 5 firms take 7–12 months just to ship any AI project, and only 5% realise AI value at scale despite heavy spend. That's precisely the empirical backing LaRocque's own 'slow disruption' rebuttal (already in the same source page) argues for — yet neither page cites the other.

**Cross-domain connection 2:** Lightfin's own creative-inspiration notes name a source that's already sitting ingested elsewhere in the wiki, unlinked. lightfin/project-optimised-jungle.md's Inspirations table credits 'Scott Galloway / Ben Evans' for the novel's capex-bubble and infrastructure-concentration theme (embodied by the oligarch character Bezant) — but the wiki already holds two ingested Ben Evans source pages covering exactly that material, ai-tech/source-evans-ai-eats-world-nov2025.md ($400bn AI capex, model commoditisation) and ai-tech/source-evans-openai-compete-feb2026.md (concentration of infrastructure value, Altman 'trading paper for hard assets'), and neither is linked from the novel's page. Worth pulling Evans's specific figures/lines directly into Bezant's or Sam Penguin's dialogue rather than writing the satire from a generic memory of the inspiration.
